items.title: Can Transformers Learn to Verify During Backtracking Search?
items.abstract: Backtracking search underlies classical constraint solvers, planners, and theorem provers. Recent transformer-based reasoning systems explore search trees over their own intermediate steps. A common training recipe fits an autoregressive next-token loss on offline solver traces. The model's input at each step is a cumulative trace of all prior decisions. The optimal continue-or-backtrack predictor depends only on the current search state, since two trajectories reaching the same state admit the same viable continuations. We show that decoder-only transformers trained on cumulative traces fail this requirement in two ways: the trace can scatter state features across many positions (scattered retrieval), and the predictor can condition on the trajectory rather than the state (history entanglement). We address scattered retrieval with localization, a trace-level fix that rewrites each decision block to expose state features locally. We address history entanglement with Selective State Attention (SSA), a fixed attention mask that enforces state-based decisions structurally without modifying training data, objective, or parameters. We focus on reactive verification, after propagation has exposed a contradiction. We test SSA on 3-SAT, graph coloring, Blocks World, and backtracking parsing. On same-state pairs that differ only in prior history, SSA emits identical decisions while a cumulative-trained causal baseline does not. Our contribution is a diagnostic of transformer behavior on serialized trajectory data, paired with a structural fix. Pretrained language models that search over their own reasoning steps may face the same failure. Our analysis opens up inference-time context clearing as a candidate way to apply the same isolation without retraining.
items.llm_evidence_en: study of transformers learning verify function during backtracking search

items.llm_tldr_en: This paper shows decoder-only transformers fail to learn correct verification during backtracking search due to scattered state features and trajectory conditioning.
items.llm_tldr_cn: 回溯搜索是经典约束求解和推理的核心，近期基于变换器的系统尝试搜索自身的中间步骤。然而，标准的下一词预测训练方式下，模型无法正确学习验证功能：其输入包含整个累积轨迹，导致状态特征分散且预测器受轨迹历史影响。本文通过理论分析和实验证明了这一缺陷，并揭示了当前方法的局限性。该工作对于发展语言模型引导的启发式搜索具有重要启示，为改进自动发现算法中的搜索验证机制提供了方向。
items.llm_tldr: 回溯搜索是经典约束求解和推理的核心，近期基于变换器的系统尝试搜索自身的中间步骤。然而，标准的下一词预测训练方式下，模型无法正确学习验证功能：其输入包含整个累积轨迹，导致状态特征分散且预测器受轨迹历史影响。本文通过理论分析和实验证明了这一缺陷，并揭示了当前方法的局限性。该工作对于发展语言模型引导的启发式搜索具有重要启示，为改进自动发现算法中的搜索验证机制提供了方向。

items.title: Automatic Discovery of Disease Subgroups by Contrasting with Healthy Controls
items.abstract: In biomedical Subgroup Discovery, practitioners are interested in discovering interpretable and homogeneous subgroups within a group of patients. In this paper, assuming that healthy subjects (i.e., controls) share common but irrelevant factors of variation with the patients, we motivate and develop a Contrastive Subgroup Discovery method, entitled Deep UCSL. By contrasting patients with controls, Deep UCSL identifies subgroups driven solely by pathological factors, ignoring common variability shared with healthy subjects. Our framework employs a deep feature extractor to learn a discriminative representation space. Mathematically, we derive a novel loss based on the conditional joint likelihood of latent clusters and patient/control labels, optimized via an Expectation-Maximization strategy alternating between subgroup inference and feature encoder updates. A regularization term further encourages representations to capture disease-specific variability while ignoring variability shared with controls. Compared to previous related works, our approach quantitatively improves the quality of the estimated subgroups, as demonstrated on a MNIST example and four distinct real medical imaging datasets. Code and datasets are available at: https://github.com/rlouiset/deep_ucsl.
items.llm_evidence_en: Automatic subgroup discovery in biomedical data
items.llm_evidence_cn: 生物医学数据中的自动子群发现
items.llm_evidence: 生物医学数据中的自动子群发现
items.canonical_evidence: 生物医学数据中的自动子群发现
items.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a contrastive subgroup discovery method that contrasts patients with healthy controls to find disease-driven subgroups.
items.llm_tldr_cn: 在生物医学子群发现中，现有方法难以排除与病人共享的健康控制因素。本文提出对比性子群发现方法Deep UCSL，通过对比病人与健康对照，识别仅由病理因素驱动的子群，忽略共同变异。该方法使用深度特征提取器学习判别表示空间，并基于条件联合似然推导训练损失。实验结果表明，Deep UCSL能够发现更具病理特异性的子群，提升了子群的解释性和临床相关性。该工作为医学中的自动发现提供了新工具。
items.llm_tldr: 在生物医学子群发现中，现有方法难以排除与病人共享的健康控制因素。本文提出对比性子群发现方法Deep UCSL，通过对比病人与健康对照，识别仅由病理因素驱动的子群，忽略共同变异。该方法使用深度特征提取器学习判别表示空间，并基于条件联合似然推导训练损失。实验结果表明，Deep UCSL能够发现更具病理特异性的子群，提升了子群的解释性和临床相关性。该工作为医学中的自动发现提供了新工具。

items.title: Building a Custom Taxonomy of AI Skills and Tasks from the Ground Up with Job Postings
items.abstract: Utilizing LLMs for automated taxonomy construction presents a clear opportunity for the comprehensive, yet efficient mapping of potentially complex domains. When contending with high volumes of rapidly growing corpora, however, it becomes unclear how to best leverage such data for optimal taxonomy construction. Taking the case of systematizing AI skills in the workplace, we use two large-scale job postings corpora to investigate key design decisions for the inclusion (or exclusion) of data points for taxonomy construction. We propose TaxonomyBuilder as a blueprint for our systematic study, with which we evaluate various configurations of custom, data-informed, and hierarchical taxonomies. We demonstrate that less data can provide more clarity: filtering inputs to TaxonomyBuilder provides better domain-specific coverage than offering unfiltered inputs to clustering and LLM-enhanced hierarchical taxonomy labeling tools.
items.llm_evidence_en: uses LLMs for automated taxonomy construction, a knowledge discovery task
items.llm_evidence_cn: 利用大模型自动构建分类法，属于知识发现任务
items.llm_evidence: 利用大模型自动构建分类法，属于知识发现任务
items.canonical_evidence: 利用大模型自动构建分类法，属于知识发现任务
items.llm_tldr_en: TaxonomyBuilder leverages LLMs to systematically construct custom taxonomies from job postings, showing less data can yield better results.
items.llm_tldr_cn: 面对大量招聘数据如何高效构建AI技能分类法？本文提出TaxonomyBuilder蓝图，利用大模型从招聘信息中自动构建层次化分类体系。通过系统实验发现，精心选择少量高质量数据比使用全部数据能得到更精确的分类结构。该方法展示了LLM在知识发现中的实用价值，为自动化领域映射提供了可复现的框架。

items.title: Advancing Mathematics Research with AI-Driven Formal Proof Search
items.abstract: Large language models (LLMs) increasingly excel at mathematical reasoning, but their unreliability limits their utility in mathematics research. A mitigation is using LLMs to generate formal proofs in languages like Lean. We perform the first large-scale evaluation of this method's ability to solve open problems. Our most capable agent autonomously resolved 9 of 353 open Erdős problems at the per-problem cost of a few hundred dollars, proved 44/492 OEIS conjectures, and is being deployed in combinatorics, optimization, graph theory, algebraic geometry, and quantum optics research. A basic agent alternating LLM-based generation with Lean-based verification replicated the Erdős successes but proved costlier on the hardest problems. These findings demonstrate the power of AI-aided formal proof search and shed light on the agent designs that enable it.
items.llm_evidence_en: LLM-based formal proof search for open mathematical problems
items.llm_evidence_cn: 利用大语言模型进行形式化证明搜索，解决开放数学问题
items.llm_evidence: 利用大语言模型进行形式化证明搜索，解决开放数学问题
items.canonical_evidence: 利用大语言模型进行形式化证明搜索，解决开放数学问题
items.llm_tldr_en: Evaluates LLM-driven formal proof search on open Erdos problems and OEIS conjectures, achieving autonomous resolution of 9/353 Erdos problems and 44/492 conjectures.
items.llm_tldr_cn: 大语言模型在数学推理中表现出色，但可靠性不足限制了其应用。本文首次大规模评估了利用LLM生成形式化证明（如Lean语言）解决开放数学问题的能力。最强大的代理自主解决了353个Erdos开放问题中的9个，以及492个OEIS猜想中的44个，成本仅为每个问题几百美元。该方法已在组合学、优化、图论、代数几何和量子光学研究中部署。结果表明，LLM生成形式化证明是推进数学研究的有效途径，尤其在自动发现数学公式方面具有巨大潜力。
items.llm_tldr: 大语言模型在数学推理中表现出色，但可靠性不足限制了其应用。本文首次大规模评估了利用LLM生成形式化证明（如Lean语言）解决开放数学问题的能力。最强大的代理自主解决了353个Erdos开放问题中的9个，以及492个OEIS猜想中的44个，成本仅为每个问题几百美元。该方法已在组合学、优化、图论、代数几何和量子光学研究中部署。结果表明，LLM生成形式化证明是推进数学研究的有效途径，尤其在自动发现数学公式方面具有巨大潜力。
items.matched_query_text: how can large language models be used for automatic discovery of mathematical formulas

items.title: LLM Retrieval for Stable and Predictable Ad Recommendations
items.abstract: Traditional ads recommendation systems have primarily focused on optimizing for prediction accuracy of click or conversion events using canonical metrics such as recall or normalized discounted cumulative gain (NDCG). With the hyper-growth of ads inventory and liquidity with generative AI technologies, the prediction stability and predictability is becoming increasingly critical. Intuitively, prediction stability and predictability can be defined to quantify system robustness with respect to minor/noisy input (ads, creatives) perturbations, the lack of which could lead to advertiser perceivable problems such as repeatability, cold start and under-exploration. In this paper, we introduce a new evaluation framework for quantifying stability and predictability of an ads recommender system, and present an online validated semantic candidate generation framework powered by fine-tuned Large Language Models (LLMs) that showed significant improvement along these metrics by fundamentally improving the semantic-awareness of the system. The approach extracts hierarchical semantic attributes from ad creatives to obtain LLM representations, which serve as the foundation for graph-based expansion, ensuring the retrieved candidates encapsulate semantic variants of an ad, guaranteeing that small creative variants from the advertiser yield consistent and explainable delivery results to the user. We tested this LLM ads retrieval framework in a large-scale industrial ads recommendation system, demonstrating significant improvements across offline and online A/B experiments, showcasing gains in both predictability and traditional performance metrics. Although evaluated in the ads stack, this is a general framework that can be applied broadly to any large-scale recommendation and retrieval systems facing similar scaling and predictability challenges.
items.llm_evidence_en: LLM retrieval for stable and predictable ad recommendations
items.llm_evidence_cn: 使用大语言模型检索实现稳定可预测的广告推荐
items.llm_evidence: 使用大语言模型检索实现稳定可预测的广告推荐
items.canonical_evidence: 使用大语言模型检索实现稳定可预测的广告推荐
items.llm_tldr_en: Proposes an evaluation framework for stability and predictability of ads recommendation systems using LLM retrieval, addressing robustness to input perturbations.
items.llm_tldr_cn: 传统广告推荐系统主要优化点击率等预测准确率指标，但忽视了预测稳定性和可预测性对系统鲁棒性的重要性。本文提出一个基于大语言模型检索的新评估框架，通过量化系统对广告和创意输入扰动的鲁棒性，定义了稳定性和可预测性的度量标准。实验表明该框架能够有效识别推荐系统的脆弱点，并通过LLM检索策略提升系统的稳定性。该工作直接满足了大语言模型增强推荐系统的需求，为广告推荐领域的可靠性研究提供了新工具。
items.llm_tldr: 传统广告推荐系统主要优化点击率等预测准确率指标，但忽视了预测稳定性和可预测性对系统鲁棒性的重要性。本文提出一个基于大语言模型检索的新评估框架，通过量化系统对广告和创意输入扰动的鲁棒性，定义了稳定性和可预测性的度量标准。实验表明该框架能够有效识别推荐系统的脆弱点，并通过LLM检索策略提升系统的稳定性。该工作直接满足了大语言模型增强推荐系统的需求，为广告推荐领域的可靠性研究提供了新工具。
